title: Final Documentation Alignment
outOfScope: Application/runtime behavior and tests unrelated to documentation facts.
Release tag creation and provider publication; reserved for WI14.
Deletion of historical evidence or execution-plan records; reserved for WI15.

sources.reason: Defines WI13 documentation alignment scope and serial lifecycle requirements.
Canonical English product boundary, lifecycle, installation, and release claims.
Chinese counterpart requiring lifecycle and boundary parity.
Japanese counterpart requiring lifecycle and boundary parity.
Canonical branch, PR, merge, and closure procedure.
Trust boundary and finite negative-test coverage claims.
Explicit enterprise-security and compliance NO-GO boundary.
Fixture execution versus not_run/not_claimed evidence boundary.

riskAssessment.reason: Changes are limited to factual documentation alignment; no runtime behavior or evidence deletion is in scope.

executionDecision.reason: User authorization covers documentation alignment, verification, PR/merge, closure, and branch cleanup.

preReviewWarnings: Review canonical lifecycle ordering, English/Chinese/Japanese parity, valid command/path references, release evidence wording, finite negative-test claims, and enterprise-security NO-GO wording.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: All targeted documentation states the canonical Work Item lifecycle through ai-close-work-item and branch/base synchronization.
English, Chinese, and Japanese user-facing guidance uses aligned product, release, fixture, and enterprise-boundary facts.
Commands, paths, guard names, release tags, and not_run/not_claimed claims are checked against the repository.
Documentation metadata and repository quality checks pass; no evidence files are deleted.
guidelines: Documentation changes remain within the declared documentation, guard-reference, and Work Item evidence scope.
Do not claim enterprise assurance, trusted identity, sandbox isolation, or provider release evidence from local repository records.
Use the lifecycle fact source and existing command names as the documentation authority.

intent.problem: WI01-WI12 added bootstrap, adoption, complexity, lifecycle facts, cross-stack validation, and delusion gates, but the documentation set must be checked for stale or contradictory workflow and boundary wording before release.
constraints: Keep changes documentation-only except Work Item evidence and generated status required by the governed workflow.
Preserve enterprise-security and compliance NO-GO language.
Keep documented not_run and not_claimed states explicit.
intent.rationale: A focused cross-language documentation alignment pass prevents the release from advertising behavior or assurance that the implementation does not provide.

rawUserRequest: 先走完当前文档的流程。用户已授权按计划逐工单执行，包含修改代码、测试、文档、PR、合并、ai-close-work-item、分支清理；妄想测试失败继续修改。

declaredIntent.summary: Align the repository's current English, Chinese, and Japanese documentation with the implemented lifecycle, trust, release, fixture, and guard facts.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: canonical Work Item branch/PR/merge/ai-close-work-item lifecycle is documented
English, Chinese, and Japanese boundary/release/fixture wording is aligned
finite guard coverage and not_run/not_claimed enterprise boundary remain explicit

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly authorized documentation, governance records, verification, PR/merge, ai-close-work-item, branch cleanup, release, and plan cleanup.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
